title: phyllanthus attenuatus subsp. incarum
intro: Phyllanthus attenuatus subsp. incarum, also known as the Incarum or Phyllanthus, is a plant species belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been found to have a potential role in the treatment of various health conditions, including liver diseases and diabetes. The health benefits associated with Phyllanthus attenuatus subsp. incarum include its ability to protect against liver damage, improve immune function, and exhibit antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Phyllanthus attenuatus subsp. incarum typically involve drying and processing the leaves and stems, which can be made into teas, capsules, or tinctures for medicinal use.
